items.docs: To launch the Financial Connections authorization flow, create a Session. The session’s client_secret can be used to launch the flow using Stripe.js.

info.name: Stripe Get   Link Account Sessions Session

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Retrieves the details of a Financial Connections Session

params.name: account_holder
params.description: If present, only return accounts that belong to the specified account holder. `account_holder[customer]` and `account_holder[account]` are mutually exclusive.

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: session
params.description: If present, only return accounts that were collected as part of the given session.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

items.docs: Returns a list of Financial Connections Account objects.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Retrieves the details of an Financial Connections Account.
Disables your access to a Financial Connections Account. You will no longer be able to access data associated with the account (e.g. balances, transactions).

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: ownership
params.description: The ID of the ownership object to fetch owners from.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.
